title: oxalis san-miguelii subsp. san-miguelii
intro: Oxalis san-miguelii subsp. san-miguelii, also known as the Mexican woodsorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high concentration of oxalic acid and other compounds, which have been reported to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Oxalis san-miguelii subsp. san-miguelii can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
